name: Big White Soft Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a measuring cup, mix the buttermilk and baking soda; set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the oil, sugar, and eggs; mix well.
3. Add the buttermilk-soda mixture and blend.
4. Then add the salt, nutmeg, vanilla, and baking powder and mix again.
5. Blend in the flour (the batter will be very runny). Cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. The next day, preheat oven to 400°F.
7. For the best results, use ungreased non-stick baking sheets.
8. Dark or shiny sheets conduct heat differently, resulting in crisp brown edges, which are not desired.
9. Use 1 heaping tablespoon of batter per cookie and place them on the sheets.
10. Liberally sprinkle more sugar on top of each cookie and dot with 3 raisins.
11. Keep batter refrigerated between bakings.
12. Bake for just 5 minutes. The cookies should be barely done, still almost white. If they are golden, remove from oven to allow further baking until desired doneness is reached.
13. Carefully remove cookies with a metal spatula to a wax paper-covered rack to cool.
14. Store in tightly covered containers or freeze; wrap each cookie individually or between layers of wax paper to prevent sticking.
